description_long: This object is a vintage-style record player featuring a wooden box base with a square profile and a light tan front panel. On top sits a black circular turntable platter, likely for holding vinyl records, with a central spindle. A metallic crank handle protrudes from the side, suggesting manual operation. Extending from the back is a curved black tonearm support, while a large, separate brass horn with a flared bell is positioned nearby, indicating it serves as the sound amplifier. The horn is detached in these views, showing its curved, polished surface and wide opening. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century gramophones, combining wood, metal, and brass elements.
description: A vintage wooden record player with a black turntable, side crank, and a large detached brass horn for sound amplification.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden box, black turntable platter, and side crank handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the crank handle and the detached brass horn lying to the right.
description_view_2: Top-down and front view showing the circular opening of the brass horn and the record player box with the crank visible.
description_view_3: Rear-side view with the horn fully separated, showing the back of the record player and the curved tonearm support.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present on top of the wooden box, the turntable platter is circular, black, and relatively small compared to the base. It has a central spindle and a smooth, matte finish, with no visible grooves or markings.
attribute_tonearm: Present, the tonearm is a curved, black metallic component extending from the back of the box. It is medium in length, with a simple, smooth finish, and appears to be resting in a raised position over the platter.
